title: QUIC CRT client-side ServerHello branch-point architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest ServerHello branch-point seam: the managed client/runtime path classifies a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt as accepted or rejected, sends rejected attempts back through the existing full-handshake path without corrupting state, and does not claim resumed-handshake success yet.
risks: If the ServerHello pre_shared_key signal is not parsed from the transcript, accepted attempts cannot be distinguished from rejected ones.
If rejection does not fall back through the existing full-handshake path, the slice would silently corrupt state or poison the bootstrap path.
If accepted attempts are treated as resumed-handshake success, the runtime would overclaim behavior that the repository has not yet proved.
If the branch outcome leaks into public API or IsSupported, the slice would widen beyond the narrow proof floor.
design_summary: The managed client/runtime path already owns opaque resumption material and can build a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt. This slice extends the server-side acceptance seam just enough to read the ServerHello pre_shared_key selection, classify the attempt as accepted or rejected, and keep the rejected branch on the existing full-handshake path. Accepted attempts are recognized but do not claim abbreviated-handshake completion, and early data remains closed.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice covers only client-side ServerHello branch-point classification for a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt. Rejected attempts must rejoin the existing full-handshake path without corrupting state. Accepted attempts are recognized but do not claim resumed-handshake success, abbreviated-handshake completion, or any 0-RTT behavior.
